also note that Morimens is generous for a gacha game, but it's still a gacha game.

in any game following Hoyo's formula, you're looking at something like 0.6% rates with hard pity at 90 pulls and a soft pity system, so ~77 pulls if you win the 50/50 and ~155 if you don't. you're getting at most 70 pulls a month in Genshin, so that's two months to guarantee a single copy and this is without constellations

in Morimens, it's 3% rates with an SSR at 30 pulls and 33% chance. after missing twice, it's guaranteed. the max pulls for one copy is 90 but the average is more like 56ish. you're also looking at 50-60ish rolls a month so you can *probably* get a copy of each new unit F2P, especially if you budget the initial influx of rolls properly. in addition, you get one free dupe each month and might get lucky with an old limited character in the standard banner. one major difference here is that you don't have to get all your dupes while the banner is up, which is particularly notable when compared to games that are behind their main version and you know all the banners so F2P players just save forever to get tons of dupes of the next OP character and ignore everything else
